prediction.reasoning: Based on typical stock volatility patterns, ICE (Intercontinental Exchange) has historically shown moderate volatility with annual volatility around 25-30%. For a 22-day period, this translates to approximately 6-8% standard deviation. The point estimate assumes slight upward drift typical of financial exchanges. Confidence intervals are constructed using Monte Carlo simulation principles with increasing width for higher confidence levels, accounting for both systematic and idiosyncratic risk factors affecting exchange operators.
